items.headline: Coherent (COHR) Is Up 7.5% After CHIPS-Backed Texas AI Photonics Expansion - Has The Bull Case Changed?
items.summary: Coherent Corp. recently signed a letter of intent to receive up to US$50 million under the CHIPS and Science Act to expand its 6‑inch indium phosphide semiconductor facility in Sherman, Texas, doubling manufacturing space, quadrupling wafer capacity, and supporting AI datacenter optical networking while creating more than 1,000 jobs. This expansion deepens Coherent’s long-running collaboration with NVIDIA and reinforces Sherman’s role as a key U.S. hub for advanced photonics and AI...
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Coherent (COHR) Wins Up To $50 Million For Texas AI Chip Expansion
items.summary: Coherent (NYSE:COHR) announced a letter of intent to receive up to US$50 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding. The funding will support expansion of its Indium Phosphide semiconductor facility in Sherman, Texas. The project is expected to double production space, increase wafer capacity by 4x, and create more than 1,000 jobs. Coherent also highlighted a deeper partnership with NVIDIA focused on AI datacenter infrastructure. For investors tracking Coherent, this CHIPS Act award and...

items.headline: Nvidia's Huang pledges AI will boost manufacturing jobs. A test will come in Texas
items.summary: Jensen Huang’s company Nvidia makes the computer chips that unleashed a revolution in artificial intelligence.  Now he's wagering that an AI buildout can revive U.S. manufacturing, pushing past limits facing science and society.  The factory will produce the material for a laser to transmit data among computer chips, allowing those chips to work as a single system with more power, speed and efficiency, according to executives who discussed the technology before the public announcement.
